page: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a bare metal server in the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 2388G processor with 8 physical cores and 16 dedicated vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz. The system features 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is provided by a 3840 GB local NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2503.49 (top 25%) and single-threaded gzip compression reaching a top-tier 36.27 GB/sec (top 10%). Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 20287.03 and a PassMark CPU score of 23271.22, both falling in the middle 50%. While PassMark memory testing ranks in the top 10% at 3390.33, large-block uncached memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read and write speeds of 36.93 GB/sec and 19.38 GB/sec respectively, placing them in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal configuration offers high resource density, combining dedicated CPU execution with a substantial 128 GB memory footprint and high-capacity local NVMe storage. The absence of virtualization overhead makes it suitable for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared hardware access. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and large local storage, this server is well-suited for database hosting, memory-intensive caching layers, and local file processing, though it may face limitations in memory-intensive uncached streaming workloads.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is an x86_64 bare metal server featuring an Intel Xeon 2388G processor with 8 physical cores, 16 dedicated vCPUs at 3.2 GHz, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. It includes 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core in the top 25% and single-threaded gzip compression in the top 10%. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth presents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% for both read and write operations. The server offers high resource density and dedicated execution without virtualization overhead, making it a solid choice for database hosting, memory-intensive caching, and local file processing workloads.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server combining strong single-core performance, 128 GB of RAM, and high-capacity local NVMe storage.
